thm:0.title: Random low-degree polynomials are unbiased (BHL12)
thm:0.summary: Classical result: random low-degree polynomials over $\mathbb{F}_2$ have small bias for uniform sources.

thm:1.title: Low-degree polynomials extract from a single source
thm:1.summary: For source $X$ with min-entropy $k$ and $d \le (1-\delta)k$, random degree-$d$ polynomial $f$ has $|\mathrm{bias}_X(f)| > 2^{-ck/d}$ with probability $\le 2^{-c \cdot C(k, \le d)}$.

cor:1.title: Low-degree polynomials extract from small families
cor:1.summary: Random low-degree polynomial is good extractor for any small family of sources.

thm:2.title: Low-degree polynomials extract from sumset sources
thm:2.summary: For $k \ge Cd(n/\varepsilon^2)^{1/\lfloor d/2 \rfloor}$, random degree-$d$ polynomial is $\varepsilon$-extractor for $(n,k)$-sumset sources. Nearly tight in polynomial error regime.

def:5.title: $(n,k)$-sumset source
def:5.summary: Distribution $X = Y + Z$ where $Y, Z$ are independent $(n,k/2)$-sources over $\mathbb{F}_2^n$.

lem:3.title: Leftover Hash Lemma
lem:3.summary: Classical LHL for strong seeded extractors.

lem:6.title: Extension of BHL12
lem:6.summary: Simple extension of Bogdanov-Huang-Lelarge bias bound.

cor:2.title: Good local source extractors
cor:2.summary: Low-degree polynomials are good extractors for local sources (improving ACGLR22).

cor:3.title: Extract from polynomial sources
cor:3.summary: Low-degree polynomials extract from polynomial sources.

cor:4.title: Extract from variety sources
cor:4.summary: Low-degree polynomials extract from variety sources.

def:12.title: Almost balanced codes
def:12.summary: Definition for codes with near-equal 0/1 weight in codewords.

def:13.title: Reed-Muller codes
def:13.summary: $RM(d,n)$ = evaluation codes of degree-$d$ polynomials over $\mathbb{F}_2^n$.

thm:3.title: Strong seeded extractors from random subcodes
thm:3.summary: Random subcodes of almost-balanced linear codes yield strong seeded extractors.

thm:4.title: Extractors from RM subcodes
thm:4.summary: Random subcodes of Reed-Muller codes are strong seeded extractors.

thm:6.summary: Low-degree polynomials disperse from sumset sources — the disperser precursor to extraction.

lem:12.summary: Structural lemma on sumset-punctured RM codes.

thm:7.summary: Impossibility result: limits on sumset extractors.

thm:8.title: Low-degree low-error two-source extractor
thm:8.summary: Two-source extractor construction from low-degree polynomials.

thm:9.title: BR15 + Polynomial Freiman-Ruzsa (GGMT23)
thm:9.summary: Key theorem combining Ben-Aroya-Regev with PFR: $(k,\rho n)$-two-source disperser with rank $\le Cn$ is also $(k'=(\rho+\gamma)n, \varepsilon=2^{-\eta n})$-two-source extractor.

lem:17.title: PR04/BR15
lem:17.summary: Lemma from Pudlak-Rodl and Ben-Aroya-Regev on bilinear complexity.

thm:10.summary: Dimension expansion result for low-degree polynomials.

def:18.title: Sumset-evasive sets and functions
def:18.summary: $f$ is $(k,\varepsilon)$-sumset-evasive if $f^{-1}(0)$ has no sumset of min-entropy $k$ with probability $1-\varepsilon$.

cor:9.title: Sumset dispersers cannot disperse from high-entropy polynomial NOBF and variety sources
cor:9.summary: Impossibility result for polynomial and variety sources via sumset-evasiveness.

thm:13.title: Existential result (Mrazović 2016)
thm:13.summary: Existential proof that sumset extractors exist via random function argument.

edges.note: Single-source extraction rests on the BHL12 bias-bound extension of Lemma 6.
Single-source extraction implies small-family extraction by union bound.
Sumset-source extraction rests on the disperser result as a stepping stone.
The disperser proof uses the sumset-punctured Reed-Muller structure of Lemma 12.
Sumset-source extraction uses the disperser-to-extractor reduction of Lemma 13.
The low-error two-source extractor rests on the PFR-based disperser-to-extractor upgrade.
The general almost-balanced-code result specializes to Reed-Muller codes.
The two-source extractor uses the dimension-expansion result for low-degree polynomials.
Sumset-evasiveness yields impossibility for certain sources.
Impossibility theorem implies strengthened CGG24 bounds.
